title: Border Wall Waste Accountability Act
plain_summary: This bill tells a government office called the Government Accountability Office to find out how much money was wasted on border wall materials. They need to look at construction materials that were bought but never used to build the wall between the US and Mexico. The study should cover the time period from January 2021 to January 2025. The office has to finish their report and give it to Congress within 90 days after this bill becomes a law.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Oversight, Investigations, and Accountability.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Border Wall Waste Accountability Act.

sections.title: Section 2  GAO Study on Cost of Unused Construction Materials for Border Wall
sections.text: Within ninety days after this law is passed, the Comptroller General of the United States (the head of the Government Accountability Office, which is the group that checks how the government spends money) must give Congress a study. This study must show the total cost of all unused construction materials that the government bought to build a border wall along the border between the United States and Mexico. The study must cover the time period from January twentieth, two thousand twenty one, to January twentieth, two thousand twenty five.
